critical_failures: State-changing without nonce
SQL query with dynamic values without $wpdb->prepare()
PHPDoc block is misplaced — it appears above the function but documents a filter, not the function itself. The function has no proper @param/@return/@since tags directly on it.
@since tag uses '{{next}}' placeholder — an unreleased/template token that should never appear in production or training code.
Uses deprecated $GLOBALS['user_ID'] which has been removed in favor of get_current_user_id() or wp_get_current_user()->ID.

Unescaped output risk: REQUEST values are sanitized with sanitize_text_field() but integer IDs like user_id, tag_ID, comment ID should be cast to int or absint(), not treated as text strings — this is semantically wrong for ID values.
Uses global $pagenow and $GLOBALS directly without proper abstraction, creating brittle global state dependency.
Missing nonce verification on state-changing handler with user input
SELECT * without LIMIT

direct_file_operations
Missing PHPDoc block entirely — function has no @since, @return, or parameter documentation despite being a public-ish method on a named class

Variable $trace, $result, and $stack are used conditionally but declared without initialization, leading to potential 'undefined variable' usage across loop iterations (e.g., $trace from a previous iteration may bleed into a subsequent one where it was not set)
Uninitialized variable reuse across loop iterations: `$trace` and `$result` from one query iteration can bleed into the next query that lacks those keys, corrupting data silently
$has_main_query is first set as a bool then immediately overwritten by wp_list_filter() result — dead assignment and confusing logic

		<input type="hidden" id="<?php echo esc_attr( self::FIELD_CAN_SHOW_ENHANCED_ATTRIBUTES_ID ); ?>"

critical_failures: Missing PHPDoc block
Form inputs without labels or ARIA

I18n: The final printf uses esc_html__() wrapping a string that contains HTML placeholders (%1$s, %3$s), which means esc_html__ will escape the anchor tags injected as arguments, producing broken output. The translation wrapper is incorrectly applied to a string that will receive raw HTML via printf — the HTML tags should not be inside the translated string passed through esc_html__().
I18n/Security: 'Yoast SEO Premium' is a hardcoded untranslated string passed directly into printf output without escaping.

Accessibility: The output HTML lacks semantic structure — the page renders headings, paragraphs, and an anchor link without ARIA roles or sufficient semantic context for screen readers. The anchor link 'Continue to Yoast SEO Premium' is not wrapped in a

or other block element and has no screen-reader-specific context.
Code Quality: The function calls exit() directly, bypassing WordPress shutdown hooks and making it untestable. This is a known anti-pattern in WordPress development.

Security: The $addon->product->name, $addon->product->slug, and $addon->product->download values come from an external API (MyYoast). While $addon->product->name is escaped via esc_html() and $output values are mapped through esc_html, the trustworthiness of the data source and whether all vectors are covered is uncertain given no sanitization before use in install_addon/activate_addon calls.
Code is truncated/incomplete — the function body is cut off mid-statement (`acf_save_post( "widget_{$widget->id}` is never closed), making this an unusable and unassessable code unit
No capability check (current_user_can()) before saving widget data; only a nonce check is present
Function is defined in global namespace without a class wrapper or namespace prefix, risking collision in WordPress's global function space
PHPDoc block uses non-standard '@type' tag and '@date' tag instead of standard '@since', '@param', '@return' only; '@param' entries are missing type hints in proper PHPDoc format
Missing input sanitization
Missing PHPDoc block

code:
function consulta() {
        $videos = get_transient( 'xml_video_sitemap_consulta' );
        if ( $videos === false ) {
            global $wpdb;
            
            $busqueda   = APGSitemapVideo::dame_busqueda();
            $videos     = $wpdb->get_results( "(SELECT id, post_title, post_content, post_excerpt, post_date
                                            FROM $wpdb->posts
                                            WHERE post_status = 'publish'
                                                $busqueda
                                                AND (post_content LIKE '%youtube.com%'
                                                    OR post_content LIKE '%youtube-nocookie.com%'
                                                    OR post_content LIKE '%youtu.be%'                              
                                                    OR post_content LIKE '%dailymotion.com%'
                                                    OR post_content LIKE '%vimeo.com%')
                                                OR (post_excerpt LIKE '%youtube.com%'
                                                    OR post_excerpt LIKE '%youtube-nocookie.com%'
                                                    OR post_excerpt LIKE '%youtu.be%'                              
                                                    OR post_excerpt LIKE '%dailymotion.com%'
                                                    OR post_excerpt LIKE '%vimeo.com%'))											
                                        UNION ALL
                                            (SELECT id, post_title, meta_value as 'post_content', post_excerpt, post_date
                                                FROM $wpdb->posts
                                                JOIN $wpdb->postmeta
                                                    ON id = post_id
                                                        AND meta_key = 'wpex_post_oembed'
                                                        AND (meta_value LIKE '%youtube.com%'
                                                            OR meta_value LIKE '%youtube-nocookie.com%'
                                                            OR meta_value LIKE '%youtu.be%'
                                                            OR meta_value LIKE '%dailymotion.com%'
                                                            OR meta_value LIKE '%vimeo.com%')
                                                WHERE post_status = 'publish'
                                                    $busqueda)
                                        UNION ALL
                                            (SELECT id, post_title, post_excerpt, post_parent, post_date
                                                FROM $wpdb->posts
                                                WHERE post_type = 'attachment'
                                                        AND post_mime_type like 'video%'
                                                        AND post_parent > 0)
                                        ORDER BY post_date DESC" ); //Consulta mejorada con ayuda de Ludo Bonnet [https://github.com/ludobonnet]
            set_transient( 'xml_video_sitemap_consulta', $videos, 24 * HOUR_IN_SECONDS );
            APGSitemapVideo::desactivar();
        }
        
        return $videos;
    }
critical_failures: SQL injection risk: dynamic values in query without $wpdb->prepare()
Unprepared SQL with dynamic values

The <label for='filter-by-post'> element is empty — it has no text content, making the associated <select> effectively unlabeled and inaccessible. This is a critical accessibility failure for a form input.

Nonce field is placed after the closing </select> rather than in a <form> container alongside the input it is meant to protect; the structural placement is misleading and the nonce is not verified at the point of reading $_GET['post'] (phpcs:ignore suppresses the warning but no verification occurs in this function)
